note: The REPO-SPECIFIC reef for the directive lens. Anchors are THIS repo's real semantic domains, each at a DISTINCT lattice coord (so prompts discriminate — no C,C collapse), each carrying its vocab (for routing), the load-bearing rules (the sorted lens), and the WINNING TEMPLATE (the Prompt-Cowboy fill-in-the-blanks that has worked here before). C,C is left empty on purpose — it was the collapse magnet. | THE ASYMMETRY (operator 2026-07-02): general-intelligence hallucination is the GENERALLY-correct answer applied to a situation where this repo is deliberately different. The reef exists to out-weigh the prior: what is usually correct is not always correct HERE — fill the rules so the lens finds them.

domains.vocab: stripe webhook payment checkout subscription invoice billing charge refund customer portal price
domains.template: Use the existing /api/stripe-webhook route. Verify the Svix/Stripe signature first. Idempotent on event id. Never trust amounts from the client.
rules: verify the webhook signature before processing
store the raw event so a re-parse can recover
idempotent by event id

domains.vocab: button css layout component page tailwind react tsx jsx homepage styling responsive hero nav footer render
domains.template: Match the surrounding component's conventions. Tailwind classes, no inline style unless needed. Keep the JSX balanced; entities for < and quotes in text.
rules: match the surrounding code's idioms
MCPHeraldicCrest required in section headers (blog)
no raw < or ≡ in JSX/MDX text

domains.vocab: migration sql schema table column supabase query index rls policy postgres select insert service-role
domains.template: Write the migration as idempotent SQL. RLS policy if a new table. Run via ./scripts/sql-copy.sh (the only approved path). Central vs CRM db — pick deliberately.
rules: only approved SQL path is ./scripts/sql-copy.sh
add RLS on new tables
central (bvhhlosblntckhwyagvp) vs user CRM db — choose deliberately

domains.vocab: blog post mdx breadcrumb crest BlogCallout ShortRank reader deep-link section heading anchor frontmatter publish drafts og share card list image encircled panel manifest strip drift-history ghost-read monologue grade 95 subtitle needs
domains.template: ShortRank A-K sections, MCPHeraldicCrest + emoji per header, BlogCallout for insights, breadcrumb footer, reader-POV ('what's in it for YOU'). Validate MDX before commit. Image = the commit tolerance panel.
rules: reader-POV first — what's in it for them
breadcrumb + crest per section (validated)
validate-mdx before commit — a 500 error/crash on a blog post is usually a forbidden character in the MDX text (triple-bar, not-equals, <NUMBER, <<), not a resolver bug
RESEARCH → BOOK → BLOG: add the book section first, quote it with deep links, verify anchors resolve in built HTML
crest VARIANTS encode meaning — never replace with 'default' (288 variants lost in the 2026-01-24 incident)
every section opens with the orthogonal-directions line + reader-facing lede; NO TABLES, prose with bold

panel wrong/missing on prod: check src/data/post-panels-index.json FIRST — a post missing from it (build-panels-index.mjs concurrent-write race, 2026-07-03) looks like staleness but a push won't fix it; run scripts/blog/build-panels-index.mjs + tests/blog/panel-index-current.test.mjs

the blog-list/OG share image is the DENSEST commit panel (bestPanelPathForSlug, src/lib/postPanels.ts) — NEVER encircled.png, a sparser excluded render (tests/blog/og-is-meaningful-commit-panel.test.mjs). Corrected 2026-07-03, this rule previously claimed the opposite.

every post carries its commit-encircles strip (drift history) at the TOP of the body — rendered automatically from post-panels/<slug>/manifest.json, one panel per edit-commit; the manifest IS the post's edit provenance, never delete it

the writing process includes the qwen ghost-read monologue gated at >=95 tri-check: qwen (weaker reader) monologues cold, Claude grades reality-vs-intent, revise sub-bar passages, re-monologue — publish only at 95+

the 6 needs live in the SUBTITLES (the mono orthogonal-directions lines), not the titles — connection, contribution, growth, uncertainty, certainty, significance in order, themed to the section; canonical-template-check.mjs is the door
voice: show the paradox, never name the feeling — banned: 'surprising/counterintuitive/remarkable'; put the two true things side by side
voice: concrete object or scene BEFORE the abstraction — the abstraction is the payoff, never the opener
voice: show the loss with the win — the honest failure mode, shown, is what makes the win credible
voice: no technique-name leaks in prose — name the map, the walk, the coordinates; never the internals
voice: density — cut any sentence that carries no mechanic, image, or turn of the paradox
voice: reader-as-hero — the sentence weight lands on the reader; 'we' rides in support

orthogonalize, don't fork
guarded by tests/delegation/no-git-branch.test.js

rules: triple-bar, not-equals, <NUMBER and << crash the MDX parser — use =, !=, prose

domains.vocab: walk ballistic recursive on-chip ply cascade column row transpose connectivity definer leaf anchor lattice depth
domains.template: pmu-onchip --ballistic --start <coord> on the reef CONNECTIVITY lattice (share row OR column). Row→column→row recursion; time-budget via --max-depth. State WHY the conventional algorithm fails before any PMU change.
rules: the walk is ALWAYS the real deterministic recursive on-chip ballistic walk on the connectivity lattice
NEVER the analytic / diffusion / BFS / Monte-Carlo shortcut — the normal way is what we are NOT doing
seed from the lit anchors; connectivity is share-row-OR-column, never a diagonal

forbidden conventional forms (anti-rules): sequential-loop walks · unguided floods · weight-sorted ranking · structural-grid substrate · unlit starts · analytic shortcuts · LLM-on-blocking-path · 12x12-block renders · size-as-drift · uninspected tiles
before any PMU change: state WHY the conventional algorithm fails here (time budget · chip substrate · semantic decomposition) — proceed only if it holds
jargon firewall: ballistic cascade · ply decay · ShortLex blocks · leaf walk · cloud · the explosion · time budget — never their conventional synonyms
new drift bitten → add the anti-rule + guard in the SAME commit as the fix (the ledger ratchet)

TREE-OF-TREES (design target, not yet built): leaves must back-propagate MASS — every deepest-ply leaf thickens its trunk (seed chain), so trunk weight shows where meaning lives; today's ply heat is flat-ish and works; a larger reef trades iteration for resolution on the speed surplus

domains.vocab: dogfood pipeline triptych render dashboard reference ingest validate screenshot cid inline reproduce drive gate gates send-gate emitter consistent nonsense artifact shipped regressed receipt blank empty degenerate suppressed trip

rules: FIND the running code first — never reinvent (pipeline.mjs → triptych-render.mjs → dashboard)
ground against public/d/lattice.png: 144x144 INTENT/REALITY/DELTA panels, NEVER a 12x12

send-gates are deterministic rows in SQLite (tc_pipeline_gates), sorted by the lane's reef — a NONSENSE artifact (empty encircled shape, 0-region panel) means the PIPELINE regressed: suppress the artifact, log the trip, and Claude steps in ONLY on the trip (repair worker, never checker)

ALL emitters consult gates through the ONE shared reader (scripts/pmu/pipeline-gates.mjs: readGate/recordTrip/passMinGate) — never inline SQL per pipeline; that is how the pipelines connect consistently
PROMPT health = receipt flags all green: sensor metal (never silent gzip-fallback), fill ≤70%, rules ≥3 sorted, template present, spec not thin, no C,C-degenerate, qwen bg liveness honest

COMMIT health = tolerance non-degenerate (gate untripped), encircled emitted with ≥1 region, from→to resolved, region stories grounded in THE COMMIT, trip-ledger EMPTY for the day — a non-empty trip ledger is a regression alarm, not noise

domains.vocab: cron launchd schedule scheduled poller daemon tick controller batch batched dedupe dedup token cost burn budget opus model pin pinned worker clock sqlite ledger exactly-once idle plist bootout bootstrap interval headless
domains.template: launchd is the clock, sh is the controller, SQLite is the queue + exactly-once ledger, claude is a model-pinned WORKER fired only after SQL proves work. One clock: scripts/controller/tick.sh (300s). New scheduled work = a stage: PULL (sh, $0) → MATCH (SQL dedup) → FIRE (ALL matches batched into ONE pinned worker) → MARK (SQL). Add the script to SCHEDULED_CLAUDE_SCRIPTS in the guard test.
rules: a scheduled claude -p MUST pin --model — inheriting the saved interactive default is the flagship-pricing foot-gun (the $665 incident)
the 'anything new?' check belongs in bash/SQLite ($0) — never spend an LLM session to discover there is no work
batch all matched commands into ONE worker dispatch — one context load amortized across N commands
exactly-once lives in SQL (state + ledger), never in the model's judgment
guarded by tests/ops/launchd-claude-cost-guard.test.js — add new scheduled claude scripts to its list (the ratchet)
cost numbers from transcripts are API-LIST-PRICE ESTIMATES, never charges — label them so (subscription pays the bill)
one clock: new periodic work is a STAGE in scripts/controller/tick.sh with its own stamp, never a new launchd plist
every emitter consults tc_pipeline_gates before sending; missing table falls back to builtin defaults so a gate can never silently disable
controller health = every tick logs all stages rc=0, $0 when idle; daily burn email arrives labeled as estimate; a missing daily email means stage 3 regressed

templates.name: incident-forensics
templates.when: burned cost tokens runaway broke regression forensics logs ledger what happened
templates.skeleton: Pull the ledgers (transcripts/ndjson) → dedup by id → rank by cost/impact → name the mechanism (not the symptom) → fix + guard in the SAME commit → report with honest numbers + estimate labels.

templates.name: ratchet-bugfix
templates.when: fix bug fails error broken test guard
templates.skeleton: State the done-when as a FAILING test first → find the running code (never reinvent) → minimal fix → guard ships in the same commit → prove via the pipeline's own receipt.

templates.name: canonical-content
templates.when: blog post write publish book chapter canonical piece
templates.skeleton: RESEARCH → BOOK section → BLOG quoting it (deep links verified) → 8-part spine in subtitles → crests/breadcrumbs → validate-mdx → qwen ghost-read ≥95 tri-check → own encircled panel as OG.

templates.name: delegation-package
templates.when: delegate room subdivide mesh bifurcate assign outside window
templates.skeleton: Author spec+context → bifurcate --from <room> --to <fitting room> --commit (NEVER a branch) → the commit-triptych receipt IS the delegation email → residue QC-routed, never dropped.

templates.name: controller-stage
templates.when: cron schedule poller daemon periodic daily tick automation
templates.skeleton: New periodic work = a STAGE in scripts/controller/tick.sh: PULL(sh $0) → MATCH(SQL exactly-once, stamp) → FIRE(batched, model-pinned worker) → MARK(SQL). Add to the cost-guard test list.

templates.name: reef-densify
templates.when: rules template lens reef routing sort misroute densify vocab
templates.skeleton: Harvest the lesson into per-domain rules (symptom vocabulary — readers arrive with the 500, not the tool name) → verify routing (routeToDomain score) + sort (fixtures rel@1) → guard the fixture.

templates.name: lab-experiment
templates.when: experiment measure baseline denominator ab arm battery prove
templates.skeleton: One-bit arms → deterministic metrics only (LLM reads the report, never grades the run) → quality and cost must CO-MOVE → append the ndjson keyed by reef version → the envelope is the ratchet.

templates.name: outreach-forwardable
templates.when: outreach lead investor carrier email pitch external send
templates.skeleton: Role-addressed forwarding PACKAGE to self (never person-addressed direct) → two docs (email + objectives) → lead with what retires the risk/discount → canonical door, grade ≥95, Ava bundle.

templates.name: receipt-evidence
templates.when: prove receipt evidence encircled panel verify show demo
templates.skeleton: The fix proves itself through the ONE pipeline: hooks-ON commit → the emailed receipt (encircled shape + from→to + LANE + stories) is the evidence — dogfood is the demo, never a mock.

templates.name: hindsight-audit
templates.when: backtest history retroactive hindsight replay past commits optimize
templates.skeleton: Replay git history through the deterministic router (~1s/120 commits, non-LLM) → ground truth = the commit's own diff paths → misses bake AUDITABLE sha-lists into rule meta → LLM only reads the report.

templates.name: trace-session
templates.when: trace decisions justify chat session retrospective explain choices happened reasoning recap
templates.skeleton: Retrospective, NOT a task: reconstruct from receipts (transcript · decisions-ledger · lens-receipts · git log) — every claim cites its receipt; decisions numbered, each with its why + evidence; NO repo edits.

templates.name: continuation
templates.when: task notification background completed status output exit code finished watcher summary daemon returned
templates.skeleton: A background task finished: read its output file FIRST, verify the receipt/exit state matches the claim, report the delta in ONE line, then advance the pipeline exactly one step or stop — never re-launch blindly.

templates.name: refine-artifact
templates.when: refine revise extend rework improve polish existing blogpost draft argue against tradeoff pros cons parts extended tighten
templates.skeleton: REVISE, not create: read the CURRENT artifact end-to-end first; preserve its invariants (crest variants · anchors · frontmatter · voice); argue for/against any restructure BEFORE doing it; one refinement pass = one commit.

templates.name: ratify-decision
templates.when: good enough perfect options confirm keep decided sure works great awesome fair agreed settled
templates.skeleton: The user is CHOOSING, not tasking: restate the decision in ONE line, record it where it governs (spec · ledger · rule), apply only the minimal delta it implies, confirm — no scope creep, no re-opening settled options.

templates.name: park-idea
templates.when: maybe half mind idea park later someday nothing change something could hmm ponder
templates.skeleton: A maybe/someday thought: do NOT execute it now. Capture it revivable-cold (self-prompt --due, or bifurcate to its room) with enough context to stand alone; confirm exactly where it is parked.
